name: Caramel-Vanilla Ice Cream
recipeIngredient: 2 cans sweetened condensed milk (14 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the sweetened condensed milk into two 8-inch pie plates.
2. Cover each pie plate with aluminum foil.
3. Place each pie plate in a larger shallow pan filled with hot water to a depth of 1/4 inch.
4. Bake at 425°F for 1 hour and 20 minutes, or until the condensed milk is thick and caramel-colored. Add hot water to the pans as needed during baking.
5. Remove the foil and let the caramelized milk cool.
6. In a large bowl, beat the eggs at medium speed of an electric mixer until thick and lemon-colored.
7. Gradually add sugar, beating until light and fluffy.
8. Add the caramelized milk, half-and-half, and whipping cream, beating constantly until the mixture is well blended.
9. Pour the caramelized milk mixture into a freezer can of a 1-1/2-gallon hand-turned or electric ice cream freezer.
10. Add milk and stir well.
11. Freeze according to manufacturer's instructions.
12. Let ripen for 1 hour.
Yield: about 1-1/2 gallons. NOTE: The recipe may be halved and prepared in a 1-gallon ice cream freezer.
